channel.title: Global Cycling Network
channel.description:
The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling.

We bring compelling content, including expert tutorials, techniques, training, racing, cutting-edge bike tech, unparalleled behind-the-scenes event coverage, humour,  entertainment, and more.

Presented by ex-pro riders, GCN offers a uniquely qualified insight into the world of cycling, and most importantly, it’s fuelled by our passionate and enthusiastic fans – everyone who makes up the GCN community. We also bring the latest and greatest tech to your attention, showcase the best places in the world to ride and get exclusive access to events and races.

Subscribe to our YouTube channel 👉 www.gcn.eu
Join this channel to get access to perks 👉
www.gcn.eu/JoinYTmemberships
Insurance: Viewers in the USA and UK can explore GCN Insurance products 👉 www.gcn.eu/GCNinsurance
Visit our website 👉 www.globalcyclingnetwork.com
Upload your hacks and bodges 👉 www.gcn.eu/gcnuploader
Check out the GCN Shop 👉 www.gcn.eu/gcnshop

channel.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 687: Why A Heavier Cyclist Is A Better Cyclist | GCN Show Ep. 687
item.itunes:title: Why A Heavier Cyclist Is A Better Cyclist | GCN Show Ep. 687
item.description: Tadej Pogačar looks unstoppable after his Strade Bianche masterclass, but is his new "beefier" physique the secret to his success? This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the data suggesting that gaining weight might actually be the next big marginal gain in pro cycling. We also check out 5 brand-new bike launches from Giant, Merida, and Bianchi, find out which bike Ed Sheeran just bought, and Dan compares his Strava times to Pogačar on the infamous Le Tolfe climb!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 686: Are Pogačar And Van Der Poel From Another Planet? | GCN Show Ep. 686
item.itunes:title: Are Pogačar And Van Der Poel From Another Planet? | GCN Show Ep. 686
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the incredible dominance of riders like Tadej Pogačar and Mathieu van der Poel, asking if they are truly "extraterrestrial" compared to the rest of the peloton. We also bring you the first look at the brand-new Shimano Tiagra groupset and discuss a controversial UCI disqualification involving bent brake levers. Plus, we celebrate a massive 1-million-bike milestone with World Bicycle Relief and unbox a very special surprise from YouTube!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 685: "Cycling Has Never Been So Cheap" (WTF?!) | GCN Show Ep. 685
item.itunes:title: "Cycling Has Never Been So Cheap" (WTF?!) | GCN Show Ep. 685
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the "prohibitive" cost of cycling and investigate if the sport is actually cheaper than ever thanks to a booming second-hand market. We also explore a bizarre new scientific study suggesting that training in a hot tub could significantly boost your FTP. Plus, Dan recounts the moment he was overtaken by a World Champion while riding flat-out, and we take a look at Shimano’s latest "self-tightening" shoe patent.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 684: Pogačar Reveals His Mind Blowing Power Zones | GCN Show Ep. 684
item.itunes:title: Pogačar Reveals His Mind Blowing Power Zones | GCN Show Ep. 684
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we dive deep into Tadej Pogačar’s recently revealed power zones and what his Strava data tells us about his world-class fitness. We also explore the launch of Sir Bradley Wiggins’ new AI coaching platform and the rise of gravel racing super-teams like the new Canyon by DT Swiss squad. Plus, we discuss the latest tech from Trek’s 50th anniversary, the controversial schedule changes for Paris-Roubaix, and our regular Hack or Bodge!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 683: The WorldTour Race Where The Pros Averaged Under 100w! | GCN Show Ep. 683
item.itunes:title: The WorldTour Race Where The Pros Averaged Under 100w! | GCN Show Ep. 683
item.description:
This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the surprisingly "easy" stages of the UAE Tour, where pro riders were recording average power outputs of less than 100 watts. We also examine a sobering new study on whether car drivers actually understand cyclist hand signals and check in on Conor Dunne’s progress (and eating habits) at the Atlas Mountain Race. Plus, we reveal the most successful race bike in history and take a look at your latest ingenious hacks and questionable bodges!

Chapters: ⏱️
00:00 - Welcome to the GCN Show!
00:45 - Tadej Pogačar & Slim Shady
01:15 - Lance Armstrong Movie News
02:27 - Racing at 100 Watts: UAE Tour
13:51 - Cycling Shorts - Atlas Mountain Race Update
18:22 - Do Drivers Understand Cyclist Signals?
25:12 - The Most Successful Race Bike Ever?
29:49 - Hack or Bodge of the Week
36:26 - Caption Competition
39:27 Comment of the week

DISCLAIMER: Conor has unfortunately had to pull out of the Atlas Mountain Race, but hang tight for the video coming soon to see what went down!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 682: Have We Reached Peak Wheel Sucker? | GCN Show Ep.682
item.itunes:title: Have We Reached Peak Wheel Sucker? | GCN Show Ep.682
item.description: In this week's GCN Show, we dive into the controversial training crash of Jonas Vingegaard and the heated debate around "wheel sucking" etiquette on public roads. We also celebrate Mathieu van der Poel's historic 8th world title and discuss the massive cleanup on Strava that saw millions of incorrectly categorized activities deleted. Plus, we look at the latest in e-bike legislation and Google’s new AI navigation features for cyclists.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 681: The Cycling Industry Has Officially Gone NUTS! | GCN Show Ep. 681
item.itunes:title: The Cycling Industry Has Officially Gone NUTS! | GCN Show Ep. 681
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we’re debating whether the cycling industry has officially lost its mind with the release of €950 3D-printed sunglasses and the growing trend of "premiumisation." We also dive into the controversial new legislation in New Jersey that could require e-bike riders to have licenses and insurance, plus a look at a revolutionary e-bike with a 1,000km range. Finally, we round up the racing action, from Mathieu van der Poel’s historic record-breaking weekend to a literal kangaroo causing chaos in the Tour Down Under peloton.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 680: Is This What We Really Want? | GCN Show Ep. 680
item.itunes:title: Is This What We Really Want? | GCN Show Ep. 680
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we take a deep dive into Van Rysel’s outrageous FTP2 concept bike and ask if it really is a glimpse into the future of performance cycling. We also explore the brand new Pinarello Grevil MX, a gravel bike designed to bridge the gap between road speed and mountain bike capability. Plus, we cover record-breaking ultra-endurance news, controversial rule changes in US gravel racing, and the release of our long-awaited Peru adventure film.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 679: Are Cyclists Burning Out Earlier Than Ever? | GCN Show Ep. 679
item.itunes:title: Are Cyclists Burning Out Earlier Than Ever? | GCN Show Ep. 679
item.description:
This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the shocking news of Simon Yates’ retirement and ask if the extreme intensity of the modern pro era is causing riders to burn out sooner than ever before. We look at the shift in training, diet, and pressure that has transformed the peloton since 2020. Plus, we cover a massive list of retirements across the industry, a bizarre 500km ride in a McDonald’s drive-thru, and a heated "copycat" controversy in the world of aero tech.

Win a ‪@Vision_Tech_Usa‬ Metron 5D Handlebar!  👉  https://gcn.eu/GCNVisionCompetitionJan 💥
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 678: Lost Fitness Over Christmas? - Do this | GCN Show Ep. 678
item.itunes:title: Lost Fitness Over Christmas? - Do this | GCN Show Ep. 678
item.description: Welcome back to the GCN Show! This week, we share six essential tips to help you get back on track after the festive break and stay consistent throughout the new year. We also cover the latest cycling news, including Bosch’s new anti-theft measures, updated 2026 pro team kits, and the surprising laws surrounding drinking and cycling.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 677: 6 Things You Need To Know About Cycling In 2026 | GCN Show Ep. 677
item.itunes:title: 6 Things You Need To Know About Cycling In 2026 | GCN Show Ep. 677
item.description: Welcome to the final GCN Show of the year, where Si and Dan dive into the trends that will shape the cycling world in 2026! We tackle the big debate: has Tadej Pogačar officially surpassed Eddy Merckx as the greatest male cyclist in history? From the rise of "gromestiques" and 32-inch mountain bike wheels to the shifting science of Zone 3 training, we’re looking ahead at what’s next for the sport. Plus, we look back at our epic adventures from the past year and feature your most ingenious (and questionable) hacks and bodges.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 676: Tadej Pogačar Just Got Even Better: Everybody Should Be Scared | GCN Show 676
item.itunes:title: Tadej Pogačar Just Got Even Better: Everybody Should Be Scared | GCN Show 676
item.description:
Welcome to a special Christmas edition of the GCN Show, where we analyze Tadej Pogačar’s "scary" early-season form after he obliterated the famous Coll de Rates KOM during a massive training ride in Spain. We also take a look at the bold new 2026 kit reveals from Ineos Grenadiers and Canyon-SRAM, plus the news of a controversial billion-dollar bike path project. Finally, we celebrate the festive season with a special wheel-themed edition of Hack or Bodge and round up the latest ultra-cycling world records.

Donate 👉  https://www.hernehillvelodrome.com/festive500
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: How Ben Healy Became The Breakaway Master
item.description: Ben Healy has quickly established himself as one of the most exciting riders in the pro peloton, known for his relentless aggression and long-range attacks. We sat down with the EF Education-EasyPost star to discuss exactly how he masters the art of the breakaway, from his "almost perfect" stage wins to his obsession with aerodynamics. Plus, Ben reveals the massive fueling numbers required to sustain his attacks and how he uses fatigue resistance to break his rivals.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 675: Does Cycling HAVE To Be Dangerous? | GCN Show Ep. 675
item.itunes:title: Does Cycling HAVE To Be Dangerous? | GCN Show Ep. 675
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we weigh up the risks of riding to ask: does cycling always have to be dangerous? We also take a first look at the new 2026 World Tour team kits, break down Tadej Pogačar's surprisingly light race schedule ahead of the Tour, and catch up on Mathieu van der Poel's return to cyclocross. Plus, we have all your usual favorites including Hack or Bodge and a fresh Caption Competition.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 674: The Mistake Most Of Us Make When Training | GCN Show 674
item.itunes:title: The Mistake Most Of Us Make When Training | GCN Show 674
item.description: In this week's GCN Show, Si and Dan discuss the dreaded "Grey Zone" and why doing too much riding at this middle intensity might be making you tired without actually making you faster. We also look at a new study linking the world's happiest countries to cycling, and discuss the controversial new documentary narrated by Lance Armstrong. Plus, stay tuned for a massive counterfeit bike bust, a strange new electric vehicle from Honda, and your Hacks and Bodges!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 673: We Can’t Believe We Used To Use These Products | GCN Show 673
item.itunes:title: We Can’t Believe We Used To Use These Products | GCN Show 673
item.description: Welcome to GCN Show 673! This week, we're taking a nostalgic trip down memory lane to discuss cycling products we happily used to ride with, but wouldn't dream of touching now. From ancient gearing to questionable accessories, join us as we explore how far cycling tech has come, alongside big news from the world of pro cycling, urban cycling, and more!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 672: Cyclists Should Be Allowed To Run Red Lights | GCN Show Ep. 672
item.itunes:title: Cyclists Should Be Allowed To Run Red Lights | GCN Show Ep. 672
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we dive into a controversial debate: is it time to legalize cyclists running red lights and stop signs? We also take a look at a bonkers new "6-in-1" GPS head unit that uses AI and inflates your tires, plus we analyze the highest VO2 Max potentially ever recorded. Finally, we discuss why over 6,000 people have signed a petition to change the route of the Tour de France.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 671: I'm 42, Should I Know Better? | GCN Show Ep. 671
item.itunes:title: I'm 42, Should I Know Better? | GCN Show Ep. 671
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, Si explains the story behind his cuts and bruises after a nasty cyclocross crash! We investigate a new report that claims cycling is the most dangerous sport in America, ahead of basketball and football. Plus, Mathieu van der Poel could be taking on an Ironman, we check out the world's first aluminum velodrome.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 670: 5 Reasons Why Gravel Suspension Is Inevitable | GCN Show
item.itunes:title: 5 Reasons Why Gravel Suspension Is Inevitable | GCN Show
item.description: Is full-suspension the inevitable future of gravel cycling? With Trek launching a new full-suspension gravel bike, the GCN Show presenters debate the five reasons why we'll all end up on one, and the arguments against. Plus, we cover the latest in cycling news, including a major bike recall, Mathieu van der Poel's 'Best Cyclist' crown, and the end of Gent-Wevelgem as we know it.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 669: Are Exhibition Races Good for Cycling? | GCN Show Ep. 669
item.itunes:title: Are Exhibition Races Good for Cycling? | GCN Show Ep. 669
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the weird world of predetermined races—should fixed post-Tour criteriums be banished to the history books, or are they a bit of fun that should be celebrated? We discuss the origins of these races, share our own experiences, and debate the end-of-season ASO-run criteriums in Singapore and Japan. Plus, we've got a new law in Canada that could make it illegal to drop your child off to school on a bike, a new massive gravel stage race, and Conor tries to sell Dan on the UK's first "boozy bike trail".
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 667: Change Is Coming Are You Ready For It? | GCN SHOW 667
item.itunes:title: Change Is Coming Are You Ready For It? | GCN SHOW 667
item.description:
The cycling world is changing fast! This week, we dive into the big debates: should electric cargo bikes share your local cycle lanes, and are full-suspension gravel bikes the future? Plus, we catch up on all the latest pro cycling drama, including the surprise end to the Strava vs. Garmin lawsuit , the latest on team mergers and closures, and some truly incredible record-breaking rides.

item.title: 666: Pogačar Finally beaten… By GCN | GCN Show 666
item.itunes:title: Pogačar Finally beaten… By GCN | GCN Show 666
item.description:
The race we've all been waiting for finally happened: Tadej Pogačar took on GCN's very own "best climber" and lawyer, Andrew Feather, at the Pogi Challenge—we've got the full breakdown of how it went! Plus, we bring you all the news from the Gravel World Championships, a look at some fascinating new cycling tech and laws, and recap a huge milestone for Pogačar's career. Tune in for the news, hacks and bodges, and an update on the Zwift Racing League teams!

item.title: 665: Is This The End Of Strava? Strava Sues Garmin | GCN Show Ep. 665
item.itunes:title: Is This The End Of Strava? Strava Sues Garmin | GCN Show Ep. 665
item.description: Strava is suing Garmin, demanding they stop selling nearly all of their fitness and cycling products - a shocking move against their biggest long-term partner. We dive into this massive lawsuit, breaking down what Strava is claiming, what Garmin is asking for, and the real-world impact this corporate conflict could have on your riding and your favorite apps. Plus, we cover huge ultra-endurance news, an unbelievable UK bike theft policy change, new tech from Specialized, and all the latest racing action!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 664: The UCI Have Unbanned Stuff! What’s Next? | GCN Show Ep. 664
item.itunes:title: The UCI Have Unbanned Stuff! What’s Next? | GCN Show Ep. 664
item.description:
The UCI has made some U-turns! This week, we dive into the rule reversal on handlebar width and the ban on time-trial helmets in road races - is it a positive step, or are they still not helping themselves? Plus, we celebrate Tadej Pogačar's historic season and discuss the surprising new full-suspension gravel bike from Trek. Finally, don't miss our reaction to the Women's World Road Race Championships upset and the latest updates from the Zwift Racing League.

Check out Project Cycle Africa 👉 https://gcn.eu/ProjectCycleAfrica

item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 663: What Separates The Good From The Great? | GCN Show Ep. 663
item.itunes:title: What Separates The Good From The Great? | GCN Show Ep. 663
item.description:
Is being a champion just about power numbers and genetics, or is there more to it? We dive into what truly separates the best in the peloton from the rest, with insights from our time as professional cyclists. We also discuss the latest cycling news, from the insane valuation of Strava to the UCI's legal challenges, and share some of the best cycling hacks and bodges submitted by you!

item.title: 662: Why Are We Obsessed With Weight When It Doesn't Actually Matter? | GCN Show Ep. 662
item.itunes:title: Why Are We Obsessed With Weight When It Doesn't Actually Matter? | GCN Show Ep. 662
item.description:
In this episode of the GCN Show, we discuss the biggest topics in cycling this week. We dive into London's cycling chaos, the weight debate in professional cycling, and why Mathieu van der Poel's mountain bike dream didn't go as planned. Stick around to find out how you can win a pair of Elite Drive 2 carbon wheels!

Ride with Dan 👉 https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/g...

item.title: 661: Proof That Riding More And Riding Slower Gets You Faster | GCN Show Ep. 661
item.itunes:title: Proof That Riding More And Riding Slower Gets You Faster | GCN Show Ep. 661
item.description:
Geraint Thomas and Ellen van Dijk bow out of professional cycling, but their long careers are a testament to the benefits of consistent training. This week, we dive into new evidence that suggests long, easy rides are the key to success and check out the latest tech from Wahoo, plus we round up all the cycling news and a new world record attempt!

item.title: 660: Is Van Der Poel About To Crash And Burn? | GCN Show Ep. 660
item.itunes:title: Is Van Der Poel About To Crash And Burn? | GCN Show Ep. 660
item.description:
Has Mathieu Van Der Poel bitten off more than he can chew? We dive into his quest to become a mountain bike world champion and whether he can succeed after a string of recent crashes. Plus, we've got the latest cycling news, a brand new giveaway, and your best hacks and bodges!

item.title: 659: 7 Things We Wish We’d Known About Cycling Sooner | GCN SHOW Ep. 659
item.itunes:title: 7 Things We Wish We’d Known About Cycling Sooner | GCN SHOW Ep. 659
item.description:
Join Si and Conor as they reflect on their cycling careers and share what they wish they'd known from the very beginning. From training tips and maintenance tricks to the unwritten rules of the road, the GCN team is talking about the lessons learned over a lifetime on two wheels. You might be surprised by what they've discovered!

item.title: 658: Does Cycling Have A Problem With Elitism And Snobbery? | GCN Show Ep. 658
item.itunes:title: Does Cycling Have A Problem With Elitism And Snobbery? | GCN Show Ep. 658
item.description: Welcome to the GCN Show! This week, we issue another apology after viewers criticized our perceived snobbery in the last episode. We ask if cycling has a wider problem with being unfriendly and elitist, and if so, what can we do about it. We also have an update on the world of cycling, including a naked bike thief and cycle lanes that were ripped out in North East England!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 657: Are Pros Ruining Gravel For The Rest Of Us? | The GCN Show Ep. 657
item.itunes:title: Are Pros Ruining Gravel For The Rest Of Us? | The GCN Show Ep. 657
item.description:
Is gravel racing becoming too extreme? In this episode of the GCN Show, Si and Dan are debating whether the sport needs rules to protect it from itself, as some racers are pushing the boundaries with controversial bike setups. We also cover a MAJOR cycling transfer, new health benefits of cycling, and reveal the winner of our caption competition. Plus, we've got a fantastic Wahoo prize bundle up for grabs!

The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling.

Our videos bring fans compelling daily content including expert tutorials, techniques, training, racing, cutting-edge bike tech, unparalleled behind the scenes event coverage, humour,  entertainment, and more.

Presented by ex-pro riders, GCN offers a uniquely qualified insight into the world of cycling, and most importantly it’s fuelled by our passionate and enthusiastic fans – everyone who makes up the GCN community. We also bring the latest and greatest tech to your attention, showcase the best places in the world to ride and get exclusive access to events and races.

Welcome to the Global Cycling Network | Inside Cycling

item.title: 656: Why My New Bike Is Dangerously Fast | The GCN Show Ep. 656
item.itunes:title: Why My New Bike Is Dangerously Fast | The GCN Show Ep. 656
item.description:
This week on The GCN Show, we're asking a serious question: are modern bikes TOO good? After a ride on his new superbike, Dan argues that the incredible speeds of today's bikes could be dangerous in the wrong hands. We debate whether you need to "pay your dues" before riding the best tech.
Plus, we've got all the latest news, including the incredible story of the new Box Hill Strava KOM, a website selling used pro kit (including Ben Healy's Tour de France skin suit), and France's massive €2 billion investment into cycling.
And you won't want to miss Story Time, as Dan finally comes clean about the time he cheated in an international bike race back in the day!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 655: Tour de France Ratings Are Down. Should We Be Worried? | GCN Show Ep. 655
item.itunes:title: Tour de France Ratings Are Down. Should We Be Worried? | GCN Show Ep. 655
item.description:
Welcome back to the GCN Show! This week, we're diving into the big story: new data shows that TV viewing figures for the Tour de France are in decline. We analyse the numbers, discuss the "Pogačar effect," and ask the crucial question: should we be worried about the future of the sport?
PLUS! We've got a mind-blowing new double Everesting World Record, a spectacular result for Conor at the world's biggest race, some good news for all you weekend warriors, and a new Penny Farthing speed record!
All that, plus your best hacks, captions, and comments!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 654: The One Record Tadej Pogačar Hasn't Broken | GCN Show Ep. 654
item.itunes:title: The One Record Tadej Pogačar Hasn't Broken | GCN Show Ep. 654
item.description:
Welcome to the GCN Show! This week, we're asking a big question: Is there still one record that Tadej Pogačar can't break? We dive deep into his incredible climbing performance on Hautacam at the Tour de France and compare it to the legendary, controversial record set by Bjarne Riis back in 1996.
We also discuss the difficult topic of doping in cycling, both past and present, and whether modern advancements in technology mean not all old records are breakable. Plus, we've got news on the best and worst states for commuting in America, a look at some super-fast (and expensive) new inner tubes, and promising news from the bike industry.
And as always, we have your best hacks and bodges, comments, and the caption competition!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 653: Is The Tour de France Still The Ultimate Test Of Endurance? | GCN Show Ep. 653
item.itunes:title: Is The Tour de France Still The Ultimate Test Of Endurance? | GCN Show Ep. 653
item.description:
This week, we're asking a controversial question: is the Tour de France still the ultimate test of human endurance? We dive into the history of the race, comparing the epic 14-hour stages of the past with the high-speed, high-intensity racing of today.
We also have the latest news from the Giro d'Italia Women, including a controversial fine for race contender Marlen Reusser. We'll check out Tom Pidcock's latest MTB win, some wild new tech from the Tour, and discuss the mind-boggling number of speeding fines issued by San Francisco's new speed cameras.
Plus, we have the first-ever video reply to a bodge, your comments, and the caption competition!
Link to the Camelbak Strava Podium Challenge https://gcn.eu/cbstr 
Do you think the Tour de France is still the ultimate endurance event? Let us know in the comments!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: 652: The Insane Speeds Of The Tour de France Are Mind Blowing | The GCN Show Ep. 652
item.itunes:title: The Insane Speeds Of The Tour de France Are Mind Blowing | The GCN Show Ep. 652
item.description:
Welcome to the GCN Show! This week, we're doing a deep dive into the truly mind-boggling speeds and power data from the chaotic opening stages of the Tour de France. Find out what it really takes to stay in the front echelon in the crosswinds, with average speeds hitting over 60 km/h on the flat!


We also have the latest news from the Women's Giro d’Italia, a roundup of all the hot new tech from the Tour (including some bikes that might be illegal next year!), and a look at two incredible ultra-endurance record attempts.


Plus, we ask if Quinn Simmons' mullet could be the future of cycling safety and, as always, we have your best hacks and bodges!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: The Next Decade Long Cycling Debate Is Here | GCN Show Ep. 651
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 651

Last week, at the world’s biggest cycling trade show (Eurobike!) we caught a glimpse at the future of bikes & cycling tech. Today, we're talking you through the trends we're seeing in cycling right now, some of which are scary!

Plus, a bumper tech round up, the Moto GP rider taking on a pro bike race, Lance Armstrong coming back to pro racing (kind of) and Conor’s dream bike. 🚲✨

item.title: How Do Unbound Records Keep Getting Smashed? | GCN Show Ep. 647
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 647

Speeds at Unbound have gone up by a whopping 35% in just 10 years. How?! We didn’t know, so we asked two-time Unbound winner, Ted King, what’s changed & whether the spirit of gravel is officially dead.

Plus, we've got someone going after Mark Beaumont's round-the-world record, a teenage sensation who’s winning more than Remco Evenepoel at the same age, and how exercise can reduce your risk of dying from cancer by a third!

😎 Hit like if you want to see Dan compete in the gravel world championships in October!

item.title: We Need To Talk About Ar**hole Cyclists | GCN Show Ep. 646
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 646

We need to talk about cyclists behaving badly... It’s not a subject we like talking about or are taking lightly, but we think it’s important. Si Richardson & Dan Lloyd delve into what's happened in terms of bad cyclist behaviour and discuss what can be done about it.

Plus, we've got a new ranking of Europe's most child-friendly cycling cities, an off-the-scale fashion faux pas from one of the world's best pro cyclists, and a man riding where no one has ever ridden before. 😎

item.description:
(Watch on Youtube here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9gR_EJw9U-A)

Conor Dunne competed in the Traka gravel race in Spain this previous weekend for what was going to be an epic GCN video. On their way home to the airport though, he and our filmmaker Liam, faced an extremely unfortunate situation. In today's show, Conor shares the story.

Plus, we bring some Wahoo news, updates from Si's rave, hack/bodge of the week and much more!

item.title: Is This Event The Future Of Road Cycling? | GCN Show Ep. 641
item.description:
This week, Si and Dan dive deep into Levi's GranFondo – an event renowned for its stunning Californian scenery but equally infamous for its challenging route. We explore the unique dynamic of this event, which successfully blends a mass participation ride with a fiercely contested, unsupported race, truly taking cycling back to its basics! Is this the ultimate test?

Alongside our main talking point, we've got a packed show covering the latest cycling news, tech, and more! Including a new 24-hour indoor cycling record, Marianne Vos' incredible lifetime contract, concerns over potential US cycling tariffs, Gary Fisher's backing of a new eBike brand, the sleek new Colnago V5RS, and Zwift's adjustable cranks.
Plus, we announce competition winners, launch a new competition for a Redshift Cockpit Bundle, pay tribute to the legendary Barry Hoban, and bring you your favourite segments

Enter the Redshift competition 👉 http://gcn.eu/GCNRedshiftCompetition
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: How A Couple Of Idiots Could Ruin Cycling For Everyone | GCN Show Ep. 640
item.description:
Is pro cycling being ruined by...fans? After some wild scenes at Paris-Roubaix, we’re talking hooligan behaviour, rider safety, and what needs to be done about this. 👀 Plus we've got a basketball legend who thinks he can help UAE Team Emirates, how we may have accidentally predicted the future, and how Alex Paton was humbled on his latest trip to the US. 🇺🇸💥

Enter here for a chance to win in our Precision Fuel & Hydration competition 👉http://gcn.eu/GCNPF&HCompetition 🥳

item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: Is Gravel Riding Just Admitting Defeat To Motorists? | GCN Show Ep. 639
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 639

Does the increasing popularity of gravel riding have anything to do with a growing fear of riding on roads? And if so, does that mean we’re admitting defeat to motorists? After a gravel ride over the weekend, Dan Lloyd got to thinking.

Plus, we’re talking about the Belgian café giving away free éclairs, how Zwift are venturing outdoors, and a HUGE amount of hot new bike tech!

Enter here for a chance to win in our Precision Fuel & Hydration competition 👉http://gcn.eu/GCNPF&HCompetition 🥳

item.title: Pro Cycling Is Faster. This is Why.
item.description:
Pro cycling speeds have gotten faster and faster. But what role has tech played in that? One of the chief architects of that evolution has been the performance engineer Jean-Paul Ballard, and we sat down with him at the Immenstadt wind tunnel in Germany to discuss all the things that make riders faster and the things that don't. As well as the things that will make us faster in the future! 

He’s a fascinating person to speak to, so we hope you enjoy our conversation!
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: Are Pogačar & Van Der Poel REALLY As Good As We Make Them Out To Be? | GCN Show Ep. 638
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 638

Pogačar and Van der Poel are getting ready to face-off at the Tour of Flanders next weekend! They're the hottest properties in men’s cycling right now, but are they ACTUALLY as good as we’re all making them out to be? Or does their legendary status have something to do with the epic "rivalry" they have against each other? Dan Lloyd & Si Richardson dive in! 

Plus, new pedal designs, a book from a former GCN presenter, and why the UCI are reversing a measure they put into pro cycling to improve safety just a few years ago...

item.title: Cycling Has A BIG Problem, But Do We Need To Worry? | GCN Show Ep. 637
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 637

Is the bike industry really in trouble? 🚴‍♂️💭 After reading some gloomy news about the bike industry in the UK, Dan Lloyd & Si Richardson asked—is this really something to worry about? Turns out, maybe not! In this week’s GCN Show, they explain why.

Plus, they've got some really interesting research about safety in numbers, some standout stats from the BEST race of the year (Milan Sanremo, according to Si) & a whole load of new tech!

item.title: Why Cycling Is The Hardest Sport In The World | GCN Show Ep. 636
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 636

Is cycling categorically the most brutal sport in the world? After last week's crashes and brutal weather at Paris-Nice and Tirreno-Adriatico, it sure seems like it!

Plus, Dan Lloyd and Conor Dunne discuss the man who averaged 268w for 24 hours, the best bike paths they've ever seen, and the incredible sight of Peter Sagan dressed up as Ken with a painted-on six pack. 🤩

item.title: Van Der Poel’s Coach Explains How You Should Be Training
item.description:
Belgian coach Kristof de Kegel has been at the forefront of professional cycling for over a decade. Now a key member of Alpecin–Deceuninck’s management team, he is involved in decision-making processes that shape the form and season direction of the likes of Mathieu van der Poel and Jasper Philipsen.
Our very own presenter, Conor Dunne, was actually coached by him once upon a time too! In this video, Conor catches up with Kristof for a full discussion on training principles, philosophies and what it takes to win in pro cycling in 2025.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: Are You Making This Fundamental Mistake In Your Cycling? | GCN Show Ep. 635
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 635

This week, Dan Lloyd & Si Richardson discuss how many of us might be making a fundamental mistake with our cycling, focusing on power not speed... We dive into why some of the most physiologically-gifted riders never get the results you’d expect, whilst others, with less power, are amongst the world’s best! 🚴‍♂️💨

Plus, we cover cycling glasses with built-in A.I, a GCN shoutout in a parliamentary debate, hack/bodge of the week, AND, an update on knitting while cycling. 🤣

item.title: The Future Of Bike Tech Might Be Fast, But It Looks Sh*t! | GCN Show Ep. 634
item.description:

The future of bike tech might be fast, but it looks awful! 🫣 Si & Dan take a look at the latest trends from a weekend of cobbles in Belgium, and discuss whether the UCI NEEDS to step in to save the day (and our eyes!).

Plus, we've got a mega Zwift competition ahead of the Zwift Academy final on Saturday, March 8, how one U.S. rider smashed the distance ridden in 24 hours, and how in Japan you can knit socks while cycling!

item.title: Why Pogačar Is So Good, Racing Makes Him Slower | GCN Show Ep. 633
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 633

This week, we're diving into Tadej Pogačar and the UAE Tour! 🚴‍♂️💥 Pogačar went on an inexplicable attack. Tactically, it made no sense—so what was the reason? Is it possible he was worried about detraining? Looking at last week's UAE Tour, that just might be the case...

Plus, Si & Dan talk new tech, Dan's Manchester to London ride, how it’s never too late to ride, & much more!

item.title: How One Man Makes Millions Trafficking Stolen Bikes & No One Can Stop Him
item.description: While investigating bike theft for a video, we spoke with Bryan Hance, a U.S.-based cybersecurity expert and co-founder of Bike Index, a nonprofit dedicated to recovering stolen bikes. With years of experience, he's seen it all—but his biggest case took nearly four years to unravel, exposing a single man behind a $2.1 million bike trafficking operation. 😳 Bryan gives us a peek into the case & reflects on what it means more generally for cycling.
item.itunes:author: Global Cycling Network (GCN)
item.dc:creator: Global Cycling Network (GCN)

item.title: This Is Why We’re Not Getting Safer Cycling | GCN Show Ep. 632
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 632

🚴 New research just dropped that might give us some optimism on improving cycling for everyone...but can we actually do anything about it? We hear from one of the authors, who explains how the "loud majority" in local activism doesn’t always reflect what most people want. What role might this play in cycling? 👀

Plus, we've got scary news on car crash prevention systems, an epic comeback story, the end of Netflix's "Unchained", and a roundup of the most hipster gravel and ultra races from last week!

item.title: The Strangest Training Hack That Might Just Work | GCN Show Ep. 631
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show 631

Could riding less—or even not at all—actually make you a better cyclist? How much can you really improve with rest? After finding a study showing someone improved their VO2 max more after taking a long break from exercise, Dan Lloyd and Si Richardson got curious. 🤔

They discuss it in today's show, plus, why they believe Tadej Pogačar might race Paris-Roubaix this year, how cycling could save your employer thousands, a major announcement with Core, Egan Bernal’s amazing first win since his crash, and more!

item.title: Should Cyclists Just Leave Some Roads To Cars? | GCN Show Ep. 630
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCNShow630

Are there roads we shouldn't ride on, even if we legally can?! In today's show, Dan Lloyd and Si Richardson discuss whether we should exercise our rights as cyclists to ride down roads that might be potentially dangerous due to motorists, or just save ourselves from the "hassle" and find quieter roads. What's the line?

Plus, we share some big news from GCN & Zwift, update you on the World Cyclocross Championships, bring you the fastest road bike in the world, and much more!

item.title: UAE's Head Of Performance Reveals Secrets Of Zone 2, Low Cadence & Altitude Training
item.description: After our recent video on pro training, we’re sharing our full, uncut interview with UAE Team Emirates’ head of performance, Jeroen Swart. Jeroen and Dan Lloyd discuss how pro cycling training has changed over the last 15 years, covering things like Zone 2, tempo training, overtraining, training volume, & the monitoring of rider metrics! 📈

item.title: A Broken Dan? The 350km Ride That MIGHT Be A Step Too Far | GCN Show Ep. 629
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 629

Thanks to your donations, Dan Lloyd is officially taking on the Manchester to London ride this June—his longest bike ride EVER! 🚴 Can he really complete 350km in just one day?! Si & Dan chat about how he’s feeling and what his prep will look like. Plus, they cover a Premier League footballer trying his hand at pro cycling, the risks of cars vs. bikes, and some good news out of NYC!

item.title: I Got Smoked By An Illegal E-bike - Does It Matter? | GCN Show Ep. 628
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 628

While cycling to GCN Megabase, Si was overtaken by what seemed to be an unrestricted e-bike travelling at quite a speed. This experience got him thinking—should unrestricted e-bikes be allowed on shared bike paths? Are they creating potential safety risks? 🚲⚡

In this episode of the GCN Show, Si and Dan dive into the growing debate around e-bike regulations. They explore possible solutions to manage their risks and discuss new laws being introduced globally. They further discuss how cycling in general has positive environmental impacts, and how certain countries are promoting and making cycling safer.

item.title: Are Things About To Ungravel? | GCN Show Ep. 627
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 627

As gravel bikes evolve and become ever closer to mountain bikes, are they actually on a path to extinction? In today's show, Si & Dan question whether the gravel bike might be facing an existential crisis. Gravel bikes have evolved so much ...and where is that evolution going? 

Plus, they discuss a $30,000 steel Condor, bid farewell to the most powerful woman in cycling, share updates on fundraising for Dan's 350km ride, comments of the week, & much more!

2025 is here, and we’ve all got exciting goals and resolutions for the year! But what should those resolutions be for a cyclist? Dan Lloyd and Conor Dunne share 4 things cyclists should be doing this year to take their riding to the next level—based on the latest modern advice and scientific knowledge.

Plus, we’ve got more bad news for the cycling industry, world records, a questionable internet hack for inflating your tires, comments of the week, and much more!

item.title: Is This The Year The Bike Industry Bounces Back? | GCN Show Ep. 625
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 625

2024 was a rollercoaster year for cycling! 🚴‍♂️🌟 From Pogačar dominating the Grand Tours to Cavendish making history and Kasia Niewiadoma triumphing at the TDF Femme Avec Zwift, there was no shortage of epic moments.

But behind the excitement, the cycling industry faced challenges, with many brands struggling to recover post-Covid. Si and Dan dive into whether 2025 could be the year the industry finds its footing again.

They also reflect on GCN’s highlights, GCN's independence, and what’s in store for the year ahead! 🎉

Make Dan do the longest ride he never wanted to do 😂 👉 https://ambitious220.ambitiousaboutautism.org.uk/fundraisers/daniellloyd/manchester-to-london-2025

item.title: If You Could Change One Thing About Cycling, What Would It Be? | GCN Show Ep. 624
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 624

We love cycling (as you know!), but in this show, Si Richardson and Dan Lloyd ask a fun question: If you could change one thing about cycling, what would it be? From small things like making bikes easier to clean, to bigger changes, like improving how non-cyclists treat cyclists, we share all the ideas presenters here at GCN came up with.

We've also got the latest cycling news, a Christmas-themed "Hack/Bodge of the Week", and much more!

item.title: Why I Was Wrong About Safety in Pro Cycling | GCN Show Ep. 623
item.description:

In this week's show, Simon Richardson and Dan Lloyd discuss safety in pro cycling, and why change is needed. Specifically, they focus on whether young pros take too many risks and what could be done to mitigate accidents. Plus, they're rating the latest WorldTour team kits, diving into Strava’s questionable A.I. "compliments", and a new award for Mark Cavendish!

item.title: Is Cycling Attractive? | GCN Show Ep. 622
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 622

Cycling might be one of the most watched sports globally, but how does it fare as a hobby in the eyes of the public? 🤔

Surprisingly, surveys suggest that cycling doesn’t even crack the top 15 most attractive hobbies. Naturally, this sparked a bit of debate at GCN Megabase, so we sent Hank out for an “independent” public survey to settle it once and for all.

Let’s just say... it didn’t quite go to plan! 🚴‍♂️😂

item.title: Cycling's New Problem: It's Too Fast | GCN Show Ep. 620
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 620

Is cycling getting too fast? After the Tour de France director raised this concern, we’re diving into the debate. Could the extreme speeds during races be causing the terrible crashes we’ve seen this year? Is this really the problem, and what can be done to make racing safer? Plus, we're sharing bad news out of Canada, a new King KOM, and how bike lanes are making roads safer for everyone. 🚦👀

item.title: The SUPERPOWER Of Knowing Your Muscle Fibre Type | GCN Show Ep. 619
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 619

Are You Training Wrong? 

New research suggests you might be! Si and Dan reveal how understanding their muscle fibre types could have made them better cyclists back in the day.

In this video, they dive into the science behind muscle fibre types – fast twitch vs. slow twitch – and debate whether every cyclist should know theirs to optimize their performance and enjoy a better riding experience.

Could this be the secret to improving your training?

Win a pair of new Elitewheels G36 Gravel wheels 👉 https://gcn.eu/GCNElitewheelsCompetition

item.title: The Pro Cycling Arms Race Nobody Is Talking About | GCN Show Ep. 617
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 617

This week, Dan & Si cover the pro cycling arms race, which is real and gaining pace! This isn't a race anymore just for the best and most talented riders, but for the best coaches, aerodynamicists, minds & equipment. Dan & Si also dive into how this arms race might not actually be good news for the rest of us though...

They also cover Mark Cavendish's aikido skills, how you can get arrested for cycling and using your phone in Japan, bike brands working to limit their carbon footprint, a competition to win a CORE heat sensor and much more!

item.title: The Fast Vs Slow Cycling Debate: What’s Cycling At Its best? | GCN Show Ep. 616
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 616

Is cycling at its best when going fast or taking it slow? Is the thrill of high speeds, being aero, and pushing your limits the most enjoyable part of cycling, or is it more about relaxing, being with oneself, and enjoying a steady ride? Conor and Si are polar opposites on this debate, but will they find common ground? 👀

Plus, they're diving into how machine learning could revolutionise bike path planning, Tadej Pogačar’s epic new contract, and why Mathieu Van Der Poel might be choosing to miss the Tour de France next year...

⏱️ Timestamps: ⏱️
00:00 - Welcome to the GCN Show! 
00:38 - This week in the world of cycling 
02:17 - Cycling fast or slow: What’s the true spirit of cycling?
10:35 - Cycling Shorts

item.description:

Can too much cycling actually be BAD for you? New research sheds light on this long-running debate...and we're diving straight in. item.title: Why The Gravel World Championships Were MUCH Harder Than You Think | GCN Show Ep. 613
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 613

The 2024 Gravel World Championships took some heat for being lacking enough “serious” gravel, but we’re here to break down why it was way tougher than you think—and why it matters!

Dan & Si also share news of what Mark Cavendish has in common with James Bond, which might not be immediately obvious. They also cover why Lachlan Morton is an even bigger legend than previously thought, and why the cycling utopia of the Netherlands is going to seem a little less idyllic if one politician gets their way…

item.description:

After Tadej Pogačar became just the third rider ever to achieve the "triple crown"—winning the Giro, Tour, and World Championships—we’re diving into his training secrets! He recently revealed them openly on Peter Attia's podcast. Spoiler alert: His power stats are unbelievable. 🤯

Plus, we’ve got news on how you could get paid for providing leads to the UCI about cheating in pro cycling, a hack/bodge using a diaper, and much more!

item.title: Why All The Hate Against Cyclists? Where Is It Coming From? | GCN Show Ep. 610
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 610

After Conor disappeared down a rabbit-hole of anti-cycling comments on a local Facebook group, we started asking if we should be concerned about this rising sentiment and if there’s anything we can do about it. What's causing such extreme anti-cycling views?

Plus, Dan & Si are covering a groundbreaking new bike from Brompton, news from the best ultra-endurance athletes in the world, and the world’s most famous triathlete deciding that he’s not going to try to win the Tour de France after all.

item.title: Is This Cycling Legend About To Do The UNTHINKABLE? | GCN Show Ep. 609
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 609

Lachlan Morton is at it again with another ultra-endurance adventure, but could this be a warm-up for something even bigger? We’re talking a possible round-the-world record attempt! So, we catch up with the current record holder, Mark Beaumont to find out what he thinks about Lachlan’s current exploits, his potential route AND ultimately whether he thinks Lachlan's got a chance of clinching the record!

Plus, Si & Dan compare Peter Sagan to Ollie Bridgewood (as one does), hear why Si thinks cyclists would ruin parkrun, and wonder if Instagram really knows what the most beautiful bike rides in the world are.

item.title: Is The Bike Industry Still In Crisis? | GCN Show Ep. 608
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 608

Is the bike industry still in crisis? Last year, we spoke to an industry expert. One year on, have things gotten better or worse? Is the industry back on an upward trend, about to make a reversal, or is it still in free fall? Dan and Si find out. Plus, they discuss electronic cycling shoes, Ford e-bikes, cross-country mountain biking, and broken climbing records!

item.title: Rim Brakes Are BACK! Here's Why. | GCN Show Ep. 605
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 605
Rim brakes are not dead, in fact, they are officially BACK. We’ll be looking at the new C68, and discussing WHY Colnago are keeping rim brakes alive.

We’ve also got drop bar MTBs, the phone that predicts your FTP from just a 4-minute test, a new CEO at Rapha, the incredible biceps of Tadej Pogačar, and the now-solved mystery of a 6-month-old bear cub found dead under a bike in Central Park 10 years ago.

item.title: E-bikes ARE A Threat, And This Is Why | GCN Show Ep. 604
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 604

E-bikes might be a threat to cycling but the reason why might not be what you think. The real issue is that powerful motorbikes are being lumped together with everyday, well-regulated e-bikes. This confusion is causing a real friction, especially on cycling paths shared with pedestrians. What's the solution?

Plus, Si & Hank give you a roundup from a busy week in ultra-endurance, bring you the latest in hot tech, and reveal why Nils Politt just joined a very exclusive list...

How much are Tour de France riders earning in 2024? And how does that compare to other athletes, in general? Dan & Conor dive in. Some of the figures are staggering!

item.title: The UNBELIEVABLE Power Outputs Of Tour De France Cyclists! | GCN Show Ep. 600
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 600

The power output that the pro cyclists are producing this Tour de France are some of the highest in recent years with some averaging more than 300 watts for 4 hours! However, this Tour has also shown us that power alone cannot guarantee great performances, as the gravel stage slowed down the peloton due to many riders not having the 'skill' to find the right lines!

Dan and Si also go on to discuss some ridiculous fines imposed by the UCI at this Tour de France!

item.title: Pro Cycling’s Got A New Problem | GCN Show Ep. 599
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 599

Pro cycling’s got a new problem: the curse of the extra boring stage! After watching the Tour de France this weekend, we started wondering whether Grand Tour sprint stages need a shakeup, why they have become so boring, what can be done about it, and if anything even needs to be done about it at all!

Dan & Si also discuss how you can get paid $1,000 to ride a bike, the return of the "Wattage Bazooka", and share a quickfire interview with Visma-Lease A Bike’s Matteo Jorgensen from his Tour de France training camp!

item.title: The Future Of GCN, What’s Next? | GCN Show Ep. 597
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 597

We’ve got some big news here at GCN this week!: We’re independent again, having bought back the majority stake in Play Sports Network. In today’s show, we’ll be letting you know what that means for us, and more importantly what it means for you.

Plus, Si & Dan discuss the cycling shoe dials heading into space with NASA, update you on Lael Wilcox’s ‘round the world record attempt, explain why Si’s got a black eye, and much more!

Participate in the TRiPEAK competition here! 👉 https://gcn.eu/GCNTripeakComp

item.title: The Endurance Marker That Separates The Best From The Rest
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 596

What is the "fourth dimension" of cycling performance, or the endurance marker that separates the best cyclists from the rest? While having a high VO2 max, and other physical markers are important, new studies suggest that having incredible fatigue resistance might be the secret to elite performance!

Si & Dan also cover the latest cycling news, including Critérium du Dauphiné, two new cycling world record attempts, why teens are choosing not to drive, hack/bodge of the week, caption competition, and much more!

item.title: Gran Fondo Fatigue: Are Sportives Worth It?
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 594

Are sportives REALLY worth it, or are they a waste of money? Is it possible to have the same epic riding experience for free these days? We dive in - weighing the pros, cons, and middle ground!

Hank & Manon also cover the latest cycling news, including the end of the Giro d'Italia, retirement announcements, the winner of our Pirelli tyre competition, and much more!

item.title: Why Is Cycling Making People So Angry?
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 591

In this week's show, we take a closer look at a recent news article suggesting that cyclists are to blame for making roads dangerous. Conor & Si also react to new laws being passed against cyclists and question why cycling is so often antagonised and why it makes people so angry!

We also cover the latest cycling news, including a lycra shorts controversy, delve into the latest bike tech, get excited about Rebound, and much more!

Participate in the Pirelli Competition here!  👉 https://gcn.eu/GCNPirelliCompetition

item.title: The Free Cycling Speed That NOBODY Is Talking About
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 590

This might be the most boring-sounding cycling topic we've ever covered,  but it's really NOT, and ...it's super important! That's right - today, we're talking about efficiency and what you can do to make yourself more efficient!

Plus, Dan & Si cover all the latest cycling news from this past week, discuss the very welcome recovery of many pro-cyclists from their recent injuries, the state of the bike industry, discuss your comments on last week's videos, and much more!

item.title: Is A Cyclist's Body A Healthy Body?
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 589

Cyclists are known for their lean physiques. To some outsiders, they might appear "skinny" and "weak", and while we know that this isn’t the case, Dan & Si still tackle the debate head-on! In this week's show they dive into whether a cyclist's physique is truly the pinnacle of fitness ...or not!

Plus, Dan & Si cover all the latest cycling news from this past week, give you a rundown of the hottest bike tech seen at Sea Otter, and much more!

item.title: Is Oxytocin the secret to the cycling buzz?
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 585

Scientists have found that cycling increases levels of the love hormone, oxytocin. How does this affect your mood? Could cycling improve your social or even love life? In cycling shorts, the first openly gay man to cycle around the globe and Mathieu van der Poel signs another 10-year Canyon deal.

item.description:

A crime against cycling fashion has been committed...Visma Lease A Bike's new aero TT helmet has landed from outer space! Is this cycling helmet taking aerodynamics too far? In cycling shorts,  Canyon has released a new e-bike, and pro bikes have got heavier...and FASTER?! 

Check out the Canyon Grizl:ON!

IMPORTANT: In the time since recording the show, the UCI has banned the Specialized head sock and said that it is looking into its own regulations on time trial helmets.

item.title: The Big Problem With E-Bikes
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 581

eBikes are gaining popularity, but what is preventing further uptake? Si thinks he has the answer. e-Bikes are just too slow. Should the speed limit be changed to match those in the US? Or is this too dangerous? In other news, the Visma Lease-A-Bike team have been greedy,  hogging 6 race wins over the weekend and a police operation saw a bike theft gang put behind (handle)bars.

item.title: How Are The Pros Getting More Powerful Than Ever?
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 580

The first Tour de France winner set a 25.7 km/h average speed, but last year the pro peloton hit 41km/h. How are pro cyclists generating more power than ever before? Si is joined by a special guest to discuss the increasing power outputs recorded in elite cycling. In cycling shorts, we announce our new wheel partner and Campagnolo has a new gravel groupset! 

In association with Elite Wheels Cycles

item.title: Why Do We Keep P*ssing Off Car Drivers?
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 579: gcn.eu/gcnshow579

Get off the road! Stop skipping the traffic! We've heard it all, but why do cyclists annoy car drivers? Dan and Si discuss 7 things cyclists do that could be irritating to other road users. In cycling shorts, Bromptom has an issue with their new factory plans, some truly ludicrous bike paths from around the world and there is a new competition!

In association with 👉 Precision Fuel & Hydration, Wahoo & Zwift

item.title: 6 Signs You’ll Live A Long Life: Does Cycling Help?
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 578: https://gcn.eu/gcnshow578

Can we predict how long we'll live? Dan and Conor discuss physiological parameters that can predict your life expectancy and how cycling can help maintain these markers, even when those grey hairs start setting in. In other news, the UCI have turned to snow sports and there's a new Olympics...on steroids? 

In association with Precision Fuel & Hydration

Enter our giveaway competition 👉 https://gcn.eu/GCNPrecisionFHComp

item.title: The Potential New Doping Practice That Stinks
item.description:
🎥 Find the show and watch it on YouTube: GCN Show Ep. 577: gcn.eu/GCNShow577

Oh poo, is another doping scandal on the horizon? Be warned this one is even more strange than worm blood doping. Si and Dan discuss the possibility of using faecal transplants to increase cycling performance. In cycling shorts, Mark Cavendish's cycling training for the season and excitement is building to the cyclocross world championships.     

In association with Precision Fuel & Hydration

Enter the competition 👉 gcn.eu/GCNPrecisionFHComp

item.title: This Is The End Of Fasted Training
item.description:
🎥 Find GCN Show 576 on YouTube! gcn.eu/GCNShow576

Is a low-carb training diet really the best for cycling performance? With some new research, we could see the end of fasted training for pro endurance athletes. Si and Dan debate restricting carbohydrates for exercise and explore the science behind cycling nutrition. In cycling shorts, we interviewed ex-pro cyclist Richie Porte and dive into the story of one man and his 10,000-day cycling streak!

item.title: Welcome To The GCN Show!
item.description:
We've been on YouTube for quite some time. So long, in fact that that at the time of recording, this is now our 576th weekly GCN show.

But we've just heard about this new platform which is called a podcast.

Yeah, we know, we're a bit late to the party. But if you want to hear the latest news, views and stories from the world of cycling, from the biggest issues that affect us all right through to the hottest takes from professional cycling, be sure to join us every week for the GCN Show!

The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling.
